matchTypes.title: Drag Drive Simulator Starter Vehicles
matchTypes.subtitle: Build a strong first garage with low-risk purchases and smarter upgrades
matchTypes.intro: Your first garage should be built for progress, not prestige. Redeem RP codes first, compare dealership inventory, and use an affordable motorcycle to learn races and tuning before moving into bigger cars.

quickFacts.label: Best early-value path
quickFacts.value: Redeem codes, buy a budget motorcycle, tune it, then upgrade

quickFacts.label: Why bikes matter early
quickFacts.value: Motorcycles are cheaper than cars and can beat expensive stock cars when tuned

quickFacts.label: Main early race hub
quickFacts.value: Sentul Track on the main map

matches.name: Best First Buy Strategy
matches.description: Redeem every active RP code first. Code rewards remove a large part of the early grind and let you start with a stronger vehicle plan.

matches.name: Why A Motorcycle Is The Best Starter Pick
matches.description: A tuned motorcycle is the strongest early value: lower cost, faster map learning, and strong drag performance once you adjust gear ratios.

matches.name: When To Move Into Cars
matches.description: Switch to cars after RP income becomes steady from races, jobs, daily quests, and hidden crates for a more balanced long-term garage.

matches.name: What To Skip At The Start
matches.description: Delay helmets, cosmetic extras, and premium-only vehicles until your core garage is stable and your launch consistency improves.

matches.name: A Clean Starter Route
matches.description: Redeem codes, compare dealerships, buy a motorcycle, test at Sentul Track, tune in free roam, then save for your first serious car.

moments.description: The original map is the easiest first shopping loop because it connects multiple dealerships, the Coffee Shop, and Sentul Track.

moments.title: Surakarta City Expansion
moments.description: Surakarta adds new roads, hidden spots, and extra dealership coverage. Explore it after learning the original map layout.

moments.title: Where To Test A Fresh Purchase
moments.description: Use Sentul Track for repeatable drag tests. Pettarani Track also supports road and drag systems for setup comparisons.

moments.title: How To Shop Efficiently
moments.description: Do not buy the first vehicle at spawn. Compare inventory first because better starter options are often one short drive away.

moments.description: Pin each shopping area with short notes: browse starters, compare bikes vs cars, test launches, then continue into expansions.

mygm.subtitle: Compare vehicle categories, access paths, and showcase models before spending RP
mygm.intro: Drag Drive Simulator has a broad garage across standard cars, motorcycles, limited releases, and premium pass categories. Use this hub to scan options before you chase upgrades.

quickFacts.label: Latest big-update vehicle note
quickFacts.value: 6 new limited special vehicles plus many new cars and motorcycles

features.title: Core Vehicle Types
features.description: The live garage is built around cars and motorcycles. Both become race-viable once you tune instead of staying stock.

features.title: Limited Special Vehicles
features.description: The latest major update added six limited special vehicles, making this a high-interest category for returning players.

features.title: Premium Vehicle Categories

features.title: Featured Public Showcase Models
features.description: Commonly highlighted models include Kawasaki ZX25R, Kawasaki ZX10R, PCX 160, RX KING, and Aerox All New Limited.

features.title: What Players Should Learn From This Hub
features.description: Price alone does not decide performance. Tune quality, launch setup, and race use case matter more than buying the most expensive option early.

myrise.title: Drag Drive Simulator Tuning Guide
myrise.subtitle: Use gear ratios and test loops to convert mid-tier vehicles into competitive drag builds
myrise.intro: Tuning is the real performance system in Drag Drive Simulator. A smart setup can transform a mid-tier vehicle, while a poor setup wastes expensive builds.

quickFacts.label: Best test flow
quickFacts.value: Tune in free roam, then validate at Sentul Track

divisions.name: How Gear Ratios Work
divisions.goal: Shorter ratios improve acceleration and launch but cap top speed earlier.
opponents: Longer ratios raise top-end speed but reduce off-line punch.

divisions.name: What A Good Drag Tune Looks Like
divisions.goal: Launch hard in lower gears, stretch cleanly through higher gears, and hit peak speed near the finish line.
opponents: Avoid topping out too early during the run.

divisions.name: Best Beginner Tuning Flow
divisions.goal: Make one change per run, test in free roam, launch again, and compare behavior.
opponents: Small gear changes are easier to track than full rebuilds.

divisions.name: Where To Test Your Tune
divisions.goal: Sentul Track is the most repeatable early test route; Pettarani Track adds another route for launch and top-end checks.
opponents: Use identical test conditions before comparing results.

divisions.name: Visual And Advanced Setup Options
divisions.goal: Livery, secondary color, rims, custom plates, and basic color edits support visual builds.
opponents: Pro Suspension and premium paint add deeper control for advanced users.

tuningOptions.name: Gear Ratio Tuning
tuningOptions.notes: Core performance tuning for drag racing

tuningOptions.name: Pro Suspension Tuning
tuningOptions.notes: Advanced control over damping and stiffness

tuningOptions.name: Advanced Paint
tuningOptions.notes: HEX color support and materials like carbon fiber, matte, glossy, and metal

tuningOptions.name: Custom Plate
tuningOptions.notes: Custom text plates for personalized builds

universeMode.title: Drag Drive Simulator Drag Racing Guide
universeMode.subtitle: Learn race hubs, drag modes, and run-building fundamentals
universeMode.intro: Learn where to race, how each drag mode works, and how to build a faster run with better launch timing and gear ratios.

quickFacts.label: Core public modes
quickFacts.value: Time Trial and Tournament Drag Race

quickFacts.label: Starter workflow
quickFacts.value: Free roam, Time Trial, then Tournament

sections.title: Race Hub
sections.content: Public drag racing is centered around Sentul Track on the main map. This is the main racing location for both practice runs and competitive RP farming.

sections.title: Time Trial Drag Race
sections.content: Time Trial is a solo mode built for tune testing. RP payout is based on the distance covered per second, which makes it the best place to test launch balance before joining a live race.

sections.title: Tournament Drag Race
sections.content: Tournament Drag Race is the competitive mode. Players in the server can enter, races start automatically every few minutes, and the winner takes the biggest share of the RP prize pool.

sections.title: What Wins Runs
sections.content: A tuned vehicle beats an expensive stock vehicle more often than most new players expect. Shorter lower gears help launch harder, while longer higher gears help you reach top speed closer to the finish line.

sections.title: Starter Strategy
sections.content: Use a budget motorcycle or starter car, test in free roam first, then move into Time Trial before joining Tournament races. This is the easiest path to learning the drag system without wasting RP.

sections.title: Server Facts
sections.content: A Drag Drive Simulator server supports up to 25 players, so race traffic stays active enough for public tournament cycles without feeling overcrowded.

referenceLinks.title: Drag Drive Simulator Map Basic Tutorial - YouTube

communityCreations.title: Drag Drive Simulator Limited Vehicles
communityCreations.subtitle: Track limited drops, showcase models, and smarter buying timing
communityCreations.intro: Track the limited side of Drag Drive Simulator, including update-driven limited drops, recent showcase vehicles, and what makes a limited worth chasing.

quickFacts.label: Current update note
quickFacts.value: Beta V1.1.3 added 6 Limited Special Vehicles

quickFacts.label: Main value
quickFacts.value: Exclusivity, event identity, and tuned drag appeal

quickFacts.label: Progression rule
quickFacts.value: Secure income and a tuned daily driver first

sections.title: Current Limited Snapshot
sections.content: The current public update notes for Beta V1.1.3 state that the game added 6 new Limited Special Vehicles, alongside more new cars and motorcycles. Limited drops are clearly tied to major update cycles.

sections.title: Why Limited Vehicles Matter
sections.content: Limited vehicles are one of the main collection drivers in the game. Players chase them for exclusivity, event identity, and stronger drag-race appeal when combined with a good tune.

sections.title: Recent Public Highlights
sections.content: Recent public showcases around Drag Drive Simulator limited content include a limited Aerox showcase, limited luxury motorcycles, and a limited event reward Vesmet bike from the holiday cycle.

sections.title: Best Time to Buy
sections.content: The strongest timing is right after a major update lands. That is when limited stock, showcase videos, and player demand all spike together.

sections.title: Smart Buying Rule
sections.content: Secure one reliable money route and one tuned daily driver first, then spend on limited vehicles. Limiteds are exciting, but tuning and RP income still decide your overall progression speed.

referenceLinks.title: Drag Drive Simulator - Rolimon's Game Page
Auto Number One! Buy and Modify the Fastest Limited Drag Bike - YouTube
Roblox Drag Drive Simulator Aerox All New Limited Showcase - YouTube
CARA DAPAT MOTOR DRAG VESMET LIMITED HADIAH EVENT NATAL - YouTube

theIsland.title: Drag Drive Simulator Map Guide
theIsland.subtitle: Understand map structure, race zones, and daily travel planning
theIsland.intro: Use this guide to understand the current public map structure, major driving zones, and where each area fits into racing, jobs, exploration, and crate routes.

quickFacts.label: Main onboarding map
quickFacts.value: Original map with dealerships, Coffee Shop, and Sentul Track

quickFacts.label: Main race location
quickFacts.value: Sentul Track with Time Trial and Tournament modes

quickFacts.label: Latest expansion
quickFacts.value: Pettarani Track in Makassar City (Beta V1.1.3)

sections.title: Main Map Overview
sections.content: The original map is still the main onboarding area. It includes multiple dealerships, the Coffee Shop, and Sentul Track, which remains the central hub for public drag racing.

sections.title: Sentul Track
sections.content: Sentul Track, also called Sentul Circuit in public guides, is the main competitive racing area. It supports both Time Trial Drag Race and Tournament Drag Race.

sections.title: Surakarta City
sections.content: Surakarta City was added in V1.11 as a full city expansion. It adds new roads, dealerships, hidden spots, and its own crate-search routes, making it the most important exploration map for midgame players.

sections.title: Pettarani Track
sections.content: The current Beta V1.1.3 update notes list Pettarani Track in Makassar City as a new map expansion. It adds both Road Race and Drag Race systems to the current map rotation.

sections.title: Important Stops
sections.content: For most players, the most important stops are spawn dealerships, the Coffee Shop for the Barista job, Sentul Track for drag racing, and Surakarta side roads for crate and exploration routes.

sections.title: Route Planning Tip
sections.content: A strong daily route combines one racing stop, one job stop, and one crate sweep. That structure gives you steady RP while also uncovering more of the map.

referenceLinks.title: Drag Drive Simulator - Rolimon's Game Page
Drag Drive Simulator Map Basic Tutorial - YouTube
Roblox Drag Drive Simulator Dealership All Location - YouTube

myfaction.title: Drag Drive Simulator Hidden Crates and Anomalies
myfaction.subtitle: Optimize crate routes and track anomaly badge milestones
myfaction.intro: Discover how crate routes and anomaly badges work, which areas are worth checking, and which progression milestones are already visible in public game data.

quickFacts.label: Crate spawn zones
quickFacts.value: Alleys, behind buildings, rooftops, and dead-end roads

quickFacts.label: Best sweep logic
quickFacts.value: Side streets first, then rooftops and dead ends

quickFacts.label: Barista chain status
quickFacts.value: Three public badges already track the anomaly route

sections.title: Hidden Crate Rules
sections.content: Hidden crates spawn on both maps and commonly appear in alleys, behind buildings, on rooftops, and at dead-end roads. They reset over time, so repeat sweeps across different sessions are worth doing.

sections.title: Best Search Pattern
sections.content: Do not stay on main roads. The most efficient sweep checks side streets first, then rooftops and dead ends, then moves from the original map into Surakarta for a second pass.

sections.title: Surakarta Crate Advantage
sections.content: Surakarta has its own separate hidden-crate spawns, which makes it more valuable than a simple expansion map. Players who only stay on the original map miss part of the crate economy.

sections.title: Anomaly Collector Progress
sections.content: Public badge data shows that Anomaly Collector: Brainrot! has been awarded 288,227 times, making it the most visible anomaly milestone in current public tracking.

sections.title: Barista Anomaly Chain
sections.content: The current barista-side anomaly chain is visible through public badges: Anomaly Barista Visitor: Unknown Entity! has 71,093 awards, Anomaly Barista Menu: Cursed Recipe! has 50,562 awards, and Barista Hunter: 13 Anomalies! has 87 awards.

sections.title: Why This Module Matters
sections.content: This is one of the best exploration modules on the homepage because it mixes hidden rewards, repeat visits, badge hunting, and route efficiency in one place.

spoilerToggle.label: Show anomaly badge details
items: Anomaly Barista Visitor: Unknown Entity! - 71,093 awards

lockerCodes.title: Drag Drive Simulator Gamepasses
lockerCodes.subtitle: Compare progression, access, and utility passes before you spend
lockerCodes.intro: These gamepasses shape progression in different ways. The strongest value picks usually improve income, unlock a new job path, open drag-spec vehicles, or expand garage capacity.

sections.title: Best Value Picks
sections.content: 2x Paycheck is the premium progression option for players who spend time on jobs, races, and other RP activities. Police Pass is the cleanest unlock for a dedicated police role with vehicles, rank progression, and mission-style play. DragSpec Pass is the focused option for straight-line performance players.

sections.title: Collection and Access Picks
sections.content: Luxury Pass opens the premium side of the vehicle catalog, while Slot Expansion Pass helps once your garage starts filling up. Slot Expansion becomes practical for limited collectors or players who keep separate builds for roleplay, jobs, and drag racing.

sections.title: What to Buy First
sections.content: For long-term progression, start with 2x Paycheck if your goal is faster RP growth. Buy Police Pass if you want a new role and rank ladder. Choose DragSpec Pass if your endgame is drag racing, and pick Slot Expansion after garage space becomes an active bottleneck.

gamepassData.name: 2x Paycheck
gamepassData.userBenefit: Doubles RP income from gameplay activities and is the strongest value pass for steady progression.

gamepassData.name: Police Pass
gamepassData.userBenefit: Unlocks the Police Job, police vehicles, rank progression, special missions, and police gear tied to promotion.

gamepassData.name: DragSpec Pass
gamepassData.userBenefit: Unlocks drag-spec vehicles built for straight-line racing and higher-performance drag setups.

gamepassData.name: Luxury Pass
gamepassData.userBenefit: Unlocks the luxury vehicle category, including high-end bikes and premium rides.

gamepassData.name: Slot Expansion Pass
gamepassData.userBenefit: Expands vehicle storage beyond the standard 75-slot cap for larger collections and more saved builds.

pcRequirements.title: Drag Drive Simulator Customization
pcRequirements.subtitle: Build unique style setups with paint, plate, stance, and visual passes
pcRequirements.intro: Customization in Drag Drive Simulator goes beyond paint. Current paid options support custom plates, advanced paint control, stance tuning, and category-based visual upgrades like rims and premium accessories.

sections.title: Core Visual Upgrades
sections.content: Custom Plate Pass lets players create personalized license plates. Advance Paint Pass expands paint control and supports more unique build identity and cleaner screenshot-ready themes.

sections.title: Stance and Fitment
sections.content: Suspension Pro Pass enables more advanced stance setups and is the most direct paid option for lowered builds, fitment-focused screenshots, and style-heavy roleplay vehicles.

sections.title: Style-Focused Progression
sections.content: Exclusive Rims Pass and Premium Accessories Pass extend the cosmetic side further. Together they provide enough variety for showcase pages, style galleries, and themed build collections.

customizationData.name: Advance Paint Pass
customizationData.userBenefit: Expands paint customization and supports more personalized vehicle color setups.

customizationData.name: Custom Plate Pass
customizationData.userBenefit: Lets players create custom license plates with their own letters and numbers.

customizationData.name: Suspension Pro Pass
customizationData.userBenefit: Adds more suspension flexibility for players who want stronger stance styling and visual tuning.

customizationData.name: Exclusive Rims Pass
customizationData.userBenefit: Unlocks extra rim options for players building style-focused cars and bikes.

customizationData.name: Premium Accessories Pass
customizationData.userBenefit: Unlocks the Premium category in the Accessories menu and gives access to premium add-ons such as winglets and other visual parts.

arenas.title: Drag Drive Simulator Updates and Events
arenas.subtitle: Follow patch highlights, event cadence, and milestone changes in one timeline
arenas.intro: Drag Drive Simulator is update-driven. Major patches and seasonal events often combine new vehicles, map expansions, limited content, cosmetic systems, and fresh redeem codes.

sections.title: Latest Major Update
sections.content: The current headline update is BETA V1.1.3. It added the Pettarani Track map expansion in Makassar City, gear ratio tuning, more accessories, six new limited special vehicles, more cars and motorcycles, five new redeem codes, revving animation updates, and new race systems in Pettarani.

sections.title: Recent Event Pattern
sections.content: The recent cadence includes Ramadan, Lunar New Year, Winter Holiday, Independence Day, Halloween, Eid-themed content, and anomaly-style side content. A rotating event block is better than a static one-note patch panel.

sections.title: Why This Module Matters
sections.content: Codes, limited vehicles, map changes, race systems, and new jobs often ship together. A compact update tracker helps players understand major changes quickly and branch into dedicated guides.

latestHighlights: Pettarani Track map expansion in Makassar City
Gear ratio tuning and more accessories
Road Race and Drag Race system in Pettarani

dlcUnlockables.title: Drag Drive Simulator Community and Private Servers
dlcUnlockables.subtitle: Use official hubs and private sessions for events, convoys, and coordinated play
dlcUnlockables.intro: Drag Drive Simulator has a strong community layer around roleplay sessions, giveaways, convoy-style play, clan activity, and update tracking. Private servers matter because they support more controlled sessions for events and showcases.

sections.title: Official Community Hubs
sections.content: The core public hubs are the ADV Gamers Team Roblox group, ADV Gamers YouTube, Drag Drive Simulator Official YouTube, and the ADV Gamers Discord server. These are the best exits for news, event notices, codes, and showcase videos.

sections.title: Private Server Use Cases
sections.content: Private servers are practical for convoy driving, screenshot sessions, friend-only roleplay, and moderated community events. Public guides also note Multi Spawn and Moderator System support in private lobbies.

sections.title: At-a-Glance Community Snapshot
sections.content: With 25-player servers and over 3.1 million Roblox group members, the game keeps a strong social loop where players track updates through community channels and return for events, jobs, codes, and limited vehicles.

privateServerFeatures: Useful for controlled screenshots and showcases

faq.title: Drag Drive Simulator
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Drag Drive Simulator

questions.question: What is Drag Drive Simulator on Roblox?
questions.answer: Drag Drive Simulator is a Roblox drag racing and roleplay experience focused on vehicles, tuning, jobs, races, and map exploration.

questions.question: How do I redeem Drag Drive Simulator codes?
questions.answer: Open the Shop cart, switch to the Redeem tab, paste a valid code, and press Redeem to receive RP instantly.

questions.question: What should beginners do first in Drag Drive Simulator?
questions.answer: Redeem active codes first, buy a practical starter vehicle, and learn basic tuning before spending on cosmetics.

questions.question: Where can I play Drag Drive Simulator and follow updates?
questions.answer: Play on the Roblox game page and follow the official Discord, Roblox group, and YouTube channel for updates.

questions.question: Which activities give the best early RP in Drag Drive Simulator?
questions.answer: Codes provide the fastest starting RP burst, then races and jobs like Courier or Barista give steady progression.

questions.question: Does Drag Drive Simulator support both cars and bikes?
questions.answer: Yes. Drag Drive Simulator includes both cars and motorcycles with different handling, tuning goals, and progression paths.
